Downtown Detroit: Prepare to be surprised
Saturday, May 4, 2024, 
 Detroit 
Sunday, May 5, 2024, 
 Detroit 
Michigan
Welcome to
 All day 
Delegates arrive at Detroit Metropolitan Wayne County Airport (DTW) 
and transfer to the Westin Book Cadillac. Suggested transportation from 
airport to hotel: Detroit Air Xpress Bus
 10:00 a.m – 4.30 p.m 
Delegates arrive/Registration 
 Hotel Westin Book Cadillac 
 2:00 p.m – 4:00 p.m. 
Guided walking tour of downtown Detroit via People Mover & Q Line 
Welcome to Downtown Detroit! Home of the #1 Public Square in the U.S., and a hub of economic 
revitalization. Witness the resurgence of innovation and entrepreneurship, with new businesses, 
startups, and developments emerging throughout the downtown area and submerge yourself in its 
vibrant and welcoming community.
 5:00 p.m – 7:00 p.m.
 
Welcome Reception, 
 Hotel Westin Book Cadillac, Founders Room 
 
• ACE Michigan Group Photo 
 
 
 Overnight, Westin Book Cadillac
 All day 
Delegates arrive at Detroit Metropolitan Wayne County Airport (DTW) 
and transfer to the 
 Westin Book Cadillac. Suggested transportation 
from airport to hotel: Detroit Air Xpress Bus

Breakfast is available from 6:30 a.m. at the 
 Hotel Westin Book Cadillac 
(breakfast is not included in the accommodation rate) Attire: business/business casual
Monday, May 6, 2024, 
 Detroit
 7:00 a.m. 
Meet in Lobby / load buses 
Hotel Westin Book Cadillac 
 7:30 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 Detroit Diesel
 8:00 a.m. 
 Detroit Diesel: Connect with the industry-leading 
original equipment manufacturer driving innovation 
in the industry. Discover how state-of-the-art engines, 
axles and transmissions are designed for optimized 
performance and efficiency. 
• Official opening of ACE Michigan 
• Welcome remarks and introductions
• Quentin Messer, Jr., Chief Executive Officer, 
Michigan Economic Development Corporation
• Alejandra Y. Castillo, Assistant Secretary 
of Commerce for Economic Development, 
Economic Development Administration (EDA), U.S. 
Department of Commerce
• Arnold Chacon, Secretary for Administration and 
Finance, Organization of American States
• Tour of four key Detroit Diesel areas of 
operation
• Axle Management Center
• Engineering Lab
• High Voltage Battery
• Heavy Duty Engine
• Remarks by Governor Gretchen Whitmer, 
Governor of Michigan
• ACE Michigan Group Photo 
 
 11:30 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 Huntington Place
 12:00 p.m. 
Huntington Place
• Lunch and Tour Huntington Place 
• Michigan’s Auto & Defense Industry 
Convergence
• Nadia Abunasser, MEDC, Federal Opportunities 
Director
 • Drew Coleman, MICHauto, Senior Director
• Mark Ignash, MEDC, Strategic Initiatives & 
Ecosystem Development Director
• Hydrogen Refueling Cart demo 
• Maura Rylander, Project Manager, Fuel Cell 
Technologies, Group Vehicle Power & Mobility, 
U.S. Army Combat Capabilities Development 
Command (DEVCOM)
• Joe Mercurio, GM, Manager of New Business 
Development, Fuel Cell Strategies & Business 
Development
 
• Panel “Michigan’s Defense Industry”
• Moderator: Vicki Rowinski, Macomb
Carrie Mead, Detroit Arsenal, Garrison Manager
• Maura Rylander, Project Manager, Ground Vehicle 
Systems Center, DEVCOM
• David Castano, Compliance & Security Officer, 
SAPA Transmission
• Panel “GEM: Global Epicenter of Mobility”
• Moderator: Christine Roeder, DRP
• Alejandra Y. Castillo, Assistant Secretary of 
Commerce for Economic Development, Economic 
Development Administration (EDA)
• Danielle Manley, TechTown
• Jeannine Grant, DRP
• ACE Michigan Group Photo 
 
 5:00 p.m. 
Transfer to 
 Detroit Institute of Arts (DIA) 
 5:30 p.m.
 Detroit Institute of Arts (DIA): Get ready to 
connect at the DIA, home to a world-class collection of 
over 65,000 works of art. Admire notable acquisitions 
like Diego Rivera’s Detroit Industry fresco cycle, 
which Rivera considered his most successful work, 
and Vincent van Gogh’s Self-Portrait. Join us at this 
reception to build connections and celebrate the 
power of art to bring people together. 
• Reception
 7:30 p.m. 
Transfer to Hotel 
 Westin Book Cadillac 
 Overnight, Westin Book Cadillac
Putting the World on Wheels. 
Collaboration opportunities for the Americas

Luggage to lobby by 6:45 a.m. 
Breakfast is available from 6:30 a.m. at the 
 Hotel Westin Book Cadillac 
(breakfast is not included in the accommodation rate) Attire: business/business casual 
Tuesday, May 7, 2024, 
 Detroit / Lansing / Grand Rapids
 6:45 a.m. 
Hotel Check out / load buses with 
luggage 
Hotel Westin Book Cadillac 
 7:00 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 Michigan State University 
(MSU)
 8:30 a.m.
 Michigan State University (MSU): Join the 
Spartan community at MSU, founded in 1855 as the 
Agricultural College of Michigan, which was the first 
land grant university in the U.S. MSU welcomes over 
50,000 students each year. Explore R&D projects at the 
Facility for Rare Isotope Beams, and discover innovative 
programs in agribusiness, life sciences, logistics and more. 
• Bus tour of agricultural science and livestock 
science facilities 
• MSU STEM Building, which was built using 
Mass Timber technologies
• Welcome and introductions
• Discussion of the STEM Building and Mass Timber 
technologies
• Planning for the future Workforce 
• Panel “Regional Economic Development 
Collaboration”
• Moderator: Bob Trezise, President & CEO, Lansing 
Economic Area Partnership
• Kerry Ebersole Singh, Chief Talent Solutions 
Officer, MEDC
• Mayor Andy Shor, City of Lansing Mayor
• Cathleen Edgerly, Executive Director, Downtown 
Lansing, Inc.
• Jeffrey Smith, MSU Director of Research Parks
 11:45 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 Peckham Industries
 12:00 p.m.
 Peckham Industries: Engage with a recognized 
leader in vocational rehabilitation and inclusive culture. 
Ranked #11 on Fortune Magazine’s Great Place to 
Work list, Peckham is an award-winning organization, 
recognized for its workforce development and quality 
employment practices. Share your expertise and 
learn from theirs as we explore innovative solutions 
for regional workforce growth and collaboration 
opportunities.
• Lunch and welcome remarks
• Michael Flowers, Peckham Board President
• Mike Zamiara, President, Niowave
• Jo Sperry, Peckham Chief Executive & Inclusion 
Officer
• Tour Peckham Industries 
• Apparel division
• Call Center division
• Group art project
• Stop by Peckham Farms on way to Grand 
Rapids
• ACE Michigan Group Photo 
 
 3:30 p.m. 
Transfer to Hotel 
 Hyatt Place, Grand 
Rapids Downtown 
 4:45 p.m.
 Check-in Hotel Hyatt Place
 5:30 p.m. 
Transfer to 
 Meijer Garden
 6:00 p.m.
 Reception Meijer Garden: Connect with 
leaders, nature, and art at the world-renowned 
sculpture and horticultural landscapes of Frederik 
Meijer Gardens & Sculpture Park. Join us to inspire new 
ideas, build connections, and cultivate collaboration in 
a breathtaking setting of stunning indoor and outdoor 
gardens and sculpture galleries.
• Welcome Remarks 
• Charles Burke, President & CEO, Frederik Meijer 
Gardens
• Performance by Nottawaseppi Huron Band of 
the Potawatomi
 7:30 p.m. 
Transfer to Hotel 
 Hyatt Place, Grand 
Rapids Downtown 
 Overnight, Hyatt Place, Grand Rapids 
Downtown 
Growing Big Ideas. From agtech research to innovative 
solutions for employment success

Breakfast is available from 6:30 a.m. and included in the accommodation rate at 
 Hyatt Place 
Attire: business/business casual 
Wednesday, May 8, 2024, 
 Grand Rapids
 7:45 a.m. 
Meet in Lobby to load buses
 8:00 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 MSU Grand Rapids 
Innovation
 8:15 a.m.
 MSU Grand Rapids Innovation: Collaborate 
with innovators and experts to shape the future of 
healthcare at the MSU Grand Rapids Innovation Park. 
A collaborative health innovation hub for biomedical 
research, bioengineering and health technology. This 
enterprising ecosystem is rich with opportunities for 
transformational health discoveries that will help 
people live better and longer.
• Panel “Medical Innovation”
• Moderator: tbd
• Norman Beauchamp, MD, EVP Health Services, 
MSU
• Kyle McGregor Ph.D., MBA, Director, Health 
Innovation, MSU
• Anthony Chang, Ph.D., Founder, CEO, BAMF 
Health
• Bob Niemiec, Managing Partner, Twisthink
• Mike VanGessel, Founder & CEO, Rockford 
Construction
• Jerry Kooiman, MPA, Asst Dean, External 
Relations & Chief External Relations Officer, MSU 
College of Human Medicine
 10:00 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 Grand Valley State University 
(GVSU)
 10:15 a.m.
 Grand Valley State University (GVSU): 
Connect with like-minded individuals and explore the 
exciting possibilities in life sciences and Medbio.
• GVSU tour and lunch 
• Welcome Remarks
• Stacie Behler, Vice President & Chief Public 
Affairs & Communications Officer
• Philomena Mantella, President GVSU
• Panel: “Engineering & Computing”
• Moderator: Stacie Behler
• Paul Plotkowski, Dean, College of Engineering, 
GVSU
• Incoming Dean, College of Computing
• Jonathan Engelsma, Director, Applied 
Computing Institute, Professor of College of 
Computing
 12:30 p.m. 
Transfer to 
 Amway
 1:00 p.m.
 Amway: Engage with Amway, the world’s largest 
direct selling company, with presence in 100 countries 
through independent business owners. Amway offers a 
vast platform for collaboration and growth in nutrition, 
beauty, personal care, and home products.
• Welcome and background on Amway
• Michael Nelson, Chief Operating and Chief 
People Officer
• Tour of two facilities that manufacture 
Amway products
• ACE Michigan Group Photo 
 
 3:00 p.m. 
Transfer to 
 Steelcase
 3:30 p.m.
 Steelcase: Join the dynamic community at Steelcase 
- Grand Rapids location. Home to more than 2,000 
employees from various backgrounds and expertise, 
buzzing with creativity and collaboration. Engage with 
designers, researchers, marketers, innovators, engineers, 
and professionals in many fields to exchange ideas and 
seek new collaboration.
 • Welcome and history of Steelcase and 
furniture manufacturing in West Michigan
 • Steelcase tour
 5:00 p.m. 
Transfer to 
 Amway Grand
 5:30 p.m.
 Reception Amway Grand: Experience the rich 
history of the Amway Grand Plaza Hotel, a Grand Rapids 
landmark. With its stunning architecture, beautifully 
restored, this iconic hotel is the perfect setting to 
connect with others and explore new partnership 
opportunities.
 
 7:00 p.m. 
Walk to Hotel 
 Hyatt Place, Grand Rapids 
Downtown 
 Overnight, Hyatt Place, Grand Rapids 
Downtown 
Advanced manufacturing know-how & medical life sciences. Sharing 
models and opportunities for SMEs and research partnerships.

Breakfast is available from 6:30 a.m. and included in the accommodation rate at 
 Hyatt Place 
Attire: business/business casual
Thursday, May 9, 2024, 
 Battle Creek | Ann Arbor | Ypsilanti | Detroit
 6:30 a.m. 
Hotel Checkout / Luggage to lobby 
/ load buses
 6:45 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 Kellogg 
 
• Michigan’s Travel & Tourism Industry: Collaborate 
with innovators and experts to shape the future of 
healthcare at the MSU Grand Rapids Innovation Park. 
A collaborative health innovation hub for biomedical 
research, bioengineering and health technology. This 
enterprising ecosystem is rich with opportunities for 
transformational health discoveries that will help people 
live better and longer.
 8:15 a.m.
 Kellogg: As a leader in the food industry, Kellogg 
is at the forefront of addressing global food security 
and sustainability challenges. Their food products are 
manufactured and marketed in over 180 countries, and it is 
one of the most recognizable brands in the world. Explore 
new possibilities and discover how collaboration can shape 
the future of food production.
• Welcome remarks
• William A. Ramos, Senior Advisor to the 
Assistant Secretary, U.S. Economic Development 
Administration
• History of Kellogg and Tour
• ACE Michigan Group Photo 
 
 9:30 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 Duncan Aviation
 10:00 a.m.
 Duncan Aviation: Connect with the largest privately 
owned business jet service provider in the world. With 
complete maintenance, repair and overhaul service 
offerings at three U.S. facilities, as well as through regional 
shops and mobile teams worldwide. Duncan Aviation 
helps business aircraft operators get the most value from 
their aircraft ownership. Session will facilitate identifying 
collaboration opportunities. 
 11:15 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 University of Michigan 
(Michigan Stadium)
 12:45 p.m.
 University of Michigan (Michigan Stadium): 
“The Big House” (UMich stadium) is the largest stadium 
in the United States with a seating capacity of more than 
107,000.
 • Lunch and Welcome Remarks
• Kelly Sexton, Associate VP of Innovation 
“Partnerships & Economic Impact; “Tech System 
Ecosystem Overview”
• Dave Wentzloff, Professor, Electrical Engineering 
& Computer Science “Semiconductors, Industry 
Engagement & Launching a Startup”
• Tour of the legendary Michigan Stadium
• ACE Michigan Group Photo 
 
 2:15 p.m. 
Transfer to 
 American Center for Mobility
 2:45 p.m.
 American Center for Mobility: Join the future of 
Mobility. Located on a historic 500-acre site in Ypsilanti, 
ACM offers a collaborative hub for innovation and 
technology. Connect with industry leaders, explore cutting-
edge test environments, and discover new possibilities in 
an advanced mobility proving ground.
• Welcome: Review what is taking place during 
“Demo Days”
• Tour American Center for Mobility 
 (bus tour with guides)
 4:45 p.m. 
Transfer to 
 Michigan Central/Newlab
 5:30 p.m.
 Michigan Central/Newlab: Explore the 30-acre 
campus of Michigan Central, a hub for collaboration 
innovation district located between the historic 
neighborhoods of Corktown and Southwest Detroit. 
Connect with a growing community of visionaries, 
innovators, and change-makers building urban 
transportation solutions that advance a more sustainable 
and equitable future.
• Tour Newlab/Michigan Central and Roof Top 
Reception at Newlab
 7:30 p.m. 
Transfer to Hotel 
 Westin Book Cadillac, 
Detroit 
 
 Overnight, Westin Book Cadillac, Detroit 
Feeding the World, Aerospace Innovation & Transportation Mobility. 
Connecting food security and value chains in strategic industries

Breakfast is available from 6:30 a.m. at the 
 Hotel Westin Book Cadillac 
(breakfast is not included in the accommodation rate) Attire: business/business casual
Friday, May 10, 2024, 
 Oakland County, Detroit
 7:15 a.m. 
Gather in Lobby / load buses
 7:30 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 FANUC
 8:00 a.m. 
FANUC: A pioneer in the world of robotics and 
automation and the global leader in CNC systems, 
robotics, and ROBOMACHINEs. Pursue collaborations 
with FANUC to upskill the workforce and prepare 
students for high-tech careers in advanced 
manufacturing. 
• Welcome Remarks
• Cynthia Hutchinson, CEO, U.S. Center for 
Advanced Manufacturing
 10:00 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 Cultural programming
 10:30 a.m.
 Cultural programming: Downtown Detroit 
Cultural Center
 11:45 a.m. 
Transfer to 
 TechTown/Wayne State 
University 
 12:15 p.m.
 TechTown/Wayne State University: Join 
the vibrant entrepreneurial ecosystem at TechTown 
Detroit. Wayne State University’s innovation hub 
empowers early-stage to growth-stage businesses 
and tech entrepreneurs through programs, 
education, and resources. Connect with like-minded 
innovators and engage with top entrepreneurs 
from Michigan and the Americas as they pitch their 
business ideas at the Innovation Exchange Coalition 
(IEC). 
• Box lunch and networking with 
entrepreneurs from Michigan and ACE 
program 
• Welcome and introductions from Wayne 
State and TechTown leadership 
• Entrepreneurship showcase programming
 3:40 p.m. 
Transfer to 
 Motown Museum
 4:00 p.m.
 Motown Museum: ACE Michigan wraps up 
at the legendary Motown Museum, the birthplace 
of Hitsville U.S.A., founded by Barry Gordy. As the 
ongoing heartbeat of the Motown legacy, it’s 
the perfect setting to come together one last 
time, to reflect on the experiences and solidify 
the connections and new partnerships made 
throughout the program.
• Welcome Remarks
• Robin Terry, CEO, Motown Museum
• Tour 
• Certificates of Recognition for Participants
• Official announcement and transition 
Ceremony of the next ACE Fall 2024 Host
• Interventions at Closing Ceremony:
• Garlin Gilchrist, Lieutenant Governor of 
Michigan
• Francisco O. Mora, Ambassador, Permanent 
Representative, Permanent Mission to the OAS, 
U.S. Department of State
• Gevorg Papoyan, Minister of Economy, Ministry 
of Economy of the Republic of Armenia
 7:30 p.m. 
Transfer to Hotel 
 Westin Book 
Cadillac, Detroit
 Overnight, Westin Book Cadillac, Detroit 
Robots & Rediscovering Detroit: Rebirth of a Great City. Urban, economic 
development models and the transformational effect of entrepreneurship
